description_long: This is a rectangular, wall-mounted air conditioner unit affixed to a red brick wall. The main body is metallic gray with a prominent front grille composed of closely spaced horizontal and vertical slats, designed for air intake and output. On the right side, several insulated pipes and cables are visible, connecting the unit to the building's infrastructure. The top surface appears slightly weathered, indicating outdoor exposure, and the unit is supported by sturdy black metal brackets. There is a small, square area on the right side that may contain a brand logo or control panel, but details are indistinct. The overall appearance is industrial and functional, typical of external HVAC units found in urban environments.
description: A metallic, wall-mounted air conditioner with a front grille and visible side piping, attached to a red brick wall. The unit is weathered and supported by black metal brackets.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full grille, main body, and mounting brackets against the brick wall.
description_view_1: Right side view highlighting the protruding pipes and cables, with the unit's depth visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing only the brick wall, with the air conditioner not visible.
description_view_3: Left side view showing the solid side panel of the unit and the mounting bracket, with no visible piping.
attribute_brand_logo: A small, faint square mark on the right side may be a brand logo, but details are unclear.
attribute_grille_pattern: Front grille features a grid of horizontal and vertical slats, forming a square mesh with tight spacing.
attribute_control_panel_location: Right side, near the top front corner.
attribute_exhaust_vent_design: Single large front vent with a grid pattern; exhaust is directed outward through the grille.
